data.title: Key deals this week: American Express, Otis, Vertiv, Wipro and more
data.description:
Here's a list of key deals reported across sectors this week:

  • American Express (AXP) agreed to acquire Hypercard, known as Hyper, to add to its AI expertise and expense management capabilities, the company said Thursday.
  • Wipro Hydraulics, a part of Wipro (WIT), has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Italy-based Indeco Ind Spa, the companies said on Thursday, marking the Indian firm’s entry into the hydraulic attachments segment.
  • ESCO Technologies (ESE) down 2.7% post-market Wednesday after saying it agreed to acquire Megger Group Ltd. from TBG AG for $2.35B, consisting of $900M in cash and ESCO equity valued at ~$1.4B.
  • Austria-based BAWAG (BWAGF) (BAWAY) is set to acquire the entire issued and to-be-issued share capital of Permanent TSB (ILPMF) (ILPMY) for ~€1.62B, or about $1.91B.
  • Activist investors Bradley L. Radoff and Michael Torok (the Radoff-JEC Group), holding a 7.6% stake in Seer, Inc. (SEER) have launched a formal bid to acquire the company for $2.25 per share in cash. The offer represents a 33% premium to the most recent close and includes a Contingent Value Right.
  • Otis Worldwide Corp. (OTIS) on Monday said it acquired a majority stake in WeMaintain, a technology-enabled elevator and escalator service company, the firms said Monday. Financial terms of the agreement weren’t disclosed.
  • Somnigroup International (SGI) has agreed to acquire Leggett & Platt (LEG) in an all-stock transaction worth ~$2.5B, based on Somnigroup's April 10 closing price.
  • Vertiv (VRT) has acquired BMarko Structures, a U.S.-based provider of custom-engineered structural fabrication. Founded in 2014, BMarko specializes in custom-engineered builds, including steel and wood frames that align well with AI factory and data center requirements.
  • Credo Technology Group (CRDO) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire DustPhotonics, a leading developer of Silicon Photonics Photonic Integrated Circuit (SiPho PIC) technology for optical transceivers, in a cash-and-stock deal.

data.title: American Airlines denies merger talks with United
data.description:
• American Airlines (AAL) announced that it had not engaged in discussions with United Airlines (UAL) regarding a merger and that it was not interested in such a deal, sending the company's stock lower in post-market trading on Friday.
  • Shares of the Texas-based carrier recorded four consecutive sessions of gains this week after Bloomberg reported on Monday that United's (UAL) CEO Scott Kirby, in discussions with senior government officials, floated the idea of a potential combination with the company.
  • "While changes in the broader airline marketplace may be necessary, a combination with United would be negative for competition and for consumers," American (AAL) said.
  • Such a transaction would not be consistent with the company's views regarding the Trump administration's policies on anticompetitive practices, the company added.

data.title: SA Asks: After a post-guidance sell-off, what's next for Netflix?
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares tumbled nearly 10% Friday, the day after the company released its Q1 earnings report along with weaker-than-expected guidance and announced that co-founder Reed Hastings would not be standing for re-election as chairman. 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Vinay Utham, Eugenio Catone, Luca Socci, and Pearl Gray Equity and Research what they thought lay ahead for the streaming giant, especially in the wake of its failed takeover bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). 

Vinay Utham: In my opinion, while NFLX Q1 revenues and EPS both beat estimates, to call the EPS a beat would be flattering—strip the $2.8B WBD termination fee out, and the underlying EPS would be closer to $0.70, which would actually be a miss. Critically, what was a bit surprising to me was that NFLX management left the FY26 guidance unchanged despite the March price hike. It also didn't help that the Q2 revenue and EPS guide both came in lighter than estimates. 

The selloff is hardly a surprise given the soft Q2 guide and the unchanged full-year outlook. It is also broadly in line with the thesis that I laid out in my Q1 preview, where I downgraded NFLX from a BUY to a HOLD, arguing that at roughly $99, the name offered no meaningful cushion for disappointment. 

Looking ahead, however, the long-term thesis remains intact, in my view. Ad revenues are on track to hit management's goal of ~$3B, and the H2 margin recovery is already baked into the pricing cadence and content amortization curve. Having said that, I still maintain my view that the risk/reward ratio only becomes favorable in the mid-$80s, subsequently making the range my accumulation zone. I am not planning to sell my NFLX shares on the back of these results, but I wouldn't be adding to them even at these levels. 

Finally, beyond NFLX, I would also keep an eye on Disney (DIS), Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), and Amazon (AMZN). More specifically, how DIS executes on its DTC margins will shape how the street looks at NFLX's own margins story. How fast the WBD-Paramount Skydance (PSKY) merger closes would have an impact on NFLX's content bidding environment. As for AMZN, it remains a quiet threat, particularly around live sports, a category that NFLX management has shown an enhanced interest in recent times, especially after the WBD saga.

Eugenio Catone: Following the Q1 2026 release, the market has punished Netflix (NFLX), bringing the stock back to ~$97 per share. The company issued a great quarter, with revenues growing by 16.2% and operating income up by 18%. However, what concerned investors was the guidance: revenues are expected to grow by “just” 13.5% in Q2 2026. 

As a Netflix shareholder, I am not worried at all about the declining growth rate; it is still in double digits, and the company has more than $6B left to complete the buyback program. I believe this drop is just an opportunity to add this streaming giant, which is getting even more diversified. In addition to TV shows and movies, the future of Netflix is expanding into other forms of entertainment, such as podcasts, live sports events, and a new standalone gaming app for kids. It might soon dominate these markets as well.

Luca Socci: Netflix's (NFLX) best decade is probably ahead of us, with three main drivers of monetization clearly laid before our eyes: subscribers, pricing, and advertising. The Q1 2026 report validated this framing since it announced that ad revenue is going to double to $3B by the end of the year. Netflix has also proven to be disciplined by walking away from the WBD (WBD) deal rather than risking overpaying for it. 

In the near term, I think the clear catalyst is Hastings's chair succession. However, I think his decision also shows that he is confident in the company's future and its management team. After all, the three years after he stepped down from his role as CEO have been incredible for the company, with margin expansion from 20% to above 30% and an increasingly convincing dominance in streaming. 

Over a longer time frame, I think that something else is coming for Netflix. It may sound speculative, but I think that in five years or so, we will see it bidding for WBD-Paramount Skydance (PSKY), or at least for the studio division of the newly forming company that has to face important financial constraints right off the bat. Therefore, sticking to my current price target of $120, I think the price drop represents an opportunity rather than a warning.

Pearl Gray Equity and Research: Netflix's (NFLX) initial post-earnings sell-off reflects the stock's general volatility and systematic trading flows. 

I don't think a succession plan and weaker-than-expected guidance warrant a dramatic sell-off, especially not for a stock secured by an underlying company with a 30%+ margin outlook, sustainable double-digit sales growth, a broadening market presence, and opportunities to develop via avenues such as ads, sports coverage, short-form clips, gaming, and emerging market reach. 

Moreover, I'd highlight the fact that we have moved into a market environment that emphasizes the trajectory of thematic pricing metrics related to earnings quality, sales growth, and stock momentum as opposed to isolated fair value models. I do not foresee Netflix losing its momentum unless either the broader market or Netflix itself suffers from a long-lasting material event.

  • Top Broadcasting Stocks

data.title: Lockheed Martin, RTX contracted for $12B naval systems sale to Germany
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT) and RTX Corporation (RTX) were named principal contractors as the U.S. State Department seeks to sell an Integrated Combat System and supporting equipment worth $11.9B to Germany.
  • The department announced on Friday it has approved the potential transaction, which will provide the European nation with AEGIS-based Integrated Combat Systems, including radars and other equipment to support up to eight ships.
  • The proposed transaction could take years to finalize, subject to congressional review and negotiations between the buyer and vendors.
  • The department has already formally informed Congress regarding the deal, which, according to the agency, helps achieve American military objectives by strengthening the security of a NATO ally and improving interoperability of its naval forces with allies, including the U.S.

data.title: Anthropic holds “productive” meeting with White House
data.description:
The White House announced the conclusion of what it called a "productive and constructive" meeting with Anthropic (ANTHRO), which has sued the government after the Pentagon labeled the artificial intelligence firm as a “supply-chain risk.”

"We discussed opportunities for collaboration, as well as shared approaches and protocols to address the challenges associated with scaling this technology," the White House said in a statement, according to BBC.

The meeting between Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and senior White House officials Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles was held on Friday. It “explored the balance between advancing innovation and ensuring safety," the White House said.

Citing a statement from Anthropic (ANTHRO), The New York Times reported that the meeting brought up “key shared priorities,” including cybersecurity and AI safety.

Earlier this month, the Amazon (AMZN) and Google-backed (GOOGL) (GOOG) startup released its most powerful AI model, Mythos, which officials believe will be crucial to safeguarding government computer networks from cybersecurity threats.

In March, Anthropic (ANTHRO) sued the Trump administration after Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth labeled it a “supply-chain risk” in a dispute over a $200M contract with the Pentagon regarding the use of AI technology in classified systems.

The meeting was a potential first step towards a compromise. A possible deal between the parties is likely to exclude the Pentagon, two U.S. officials said.

data.title: ICICI Bank beats on Q4 earnings after strong credit growth
data.description:
ICICI Bank Limited (IBN) reported better-than-expected earnings with its Q4 results for fiscal 2026 on Saturday as India’s second-largest private lender recorded a notable expansion in its credit portfolio.

The Mumbai-based company said that its profit after tax for the quarter ending March 31 rose 8.5% YoY to ₹137.0B ($1.4B) compared to ₹127.0B projected by analysts, according to Bloomberg data.

Its net interest income, the difference between interest revenue and interest expense, jumped 8.4% YoY to ₹229.8B ($2.4B), while its total loan portfolio and deposits rose 15.8% YoY and ~11.4% YoY to reach ₹15.5T ($163.9B) and ₹18.0T ($189.2B), respectively.

“On credit growth, we were expecting a slightly lower number sequentially, around 4.5% to 5%, but the actual numbers are at around 6% on a sequential basis and 15.8% on a yearly basis,” remarked Sameer Sawant, Research Analyst at Mirae Asset Sharekhan.

“So that is a clear beat on our expectations as well. Otherwise also, on the operating performance front, margins were expected to remain largely the same. There is a 2-basis-point improvement, which is a positive from our expectations,” he added, according to CNBC TV18.

While ICICI’s (IBN) net interest margin for the quarter reached 4.32%, up from 4.30% in the preceding quarter, the provisions for the period (excluding tax provisions) fell about ₹1B ($10M) from ₹8.9B ($94M) in the prior-year period.

“The provision number has been much lower than what we had anticipated. Owing to that, there has been a handsome beat on profitability for ICICI Bank,” Sawant noted.

data.title: Real estate stocks surge alongside broader markets
data.description:
Real estate stocks surged alongside broader markets in a generally strong week.

Wall Street's major averages posted big weekly gains, surging to new highs for three consecutive trading days, as positive developments in the Middle East—including a temporary ceasefire deal between the involved countries—raised investors' enthusiasm.

The week also saw investors shrug off fears of a potential bubble in the AI industry, doubling down on the technology. Around $242B, or 80% of the total global venture funding in Q1, went to AI companies, Crunchbase data showed.

Long-term treasury yields fell amid optimism surrounding the economy, with the 10-year bond yield (US10Y) decreasing 9 basis points to 4.25%, the 20-year bond yield (US20Y) down 5 bps to 4.85%, and the 30-year bond yield (US30Y) retreating 1 bps to 4.91%.

Long-term mortgage rates declined to a four-week low.

The S&P 500 Real Estate Index Sector (SP500-60) rose 3.83% to 283.01 points, while the accompanying State Street Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLRE) advanced 3.88% to $44.48. The broader S&P 500 index outperformed with a 4.54% increase to 7,126.07 points.

The Dow Jones REIT Indx Equity REIT Total Return Index (REIT:IND) jumped 3.61%, while the FTSE Nareit All Equity REITs index was up 3.54%.

Weekly Winners & Losers

Among largecap stocks, BXP (BXP) led the weekly gainers. The stock added 9.53% during the course of the week to close at $58.05.

This week, the office REIT announced over 200K sq. ft. of new leases in San Francisco, highlighting solid momentum amid rising demand for premier offices.

Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL) followed, gaining 9.03% to $346.43. The company filed its annual report this week, elaborating on its Accelerate 2030 strategy.

CoStar Group (CSGP) (+8.88% W/W to $39.72) and Iron Mountain (IRM) (+7.95% W/W to $118.07) were the next on the list.

SBA Communications (SBAC) was the only stock that posted negative returns in the category. The stock retreated 0.27% to $223.14.

Last week, the telecom tower REIT is said to have seen buyout interests from the funds managed by KKR and Brookfield.

For the midcap stocks, Fermi (FRMI) (+34.50% W/W to $6.55) and Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) (+22.45% W/W to $5.29) stood out as winners.

Ironically, Fermi announced the departure of CEO Toby Neugebauer on Friday. On the other hand, Opendoor reported insider sales by COO Giang Nguyen and Chief Legal Officer Sydney Schaub.

Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) (+14.13% W/W to $48.63) and Compass (COMP) (+14.01% W/W to $8.14) were the notable gainers in the category.

National Health Investors (NHI) (-0.96% W/W to $85.65), CareTrust REIT (CTRE) (-0.48% W/W to $39.50), and ARMOUR Residential REIT (ARR) (-0.17% W/W to $17.64) were the losers.

Among smallcap stocks, Hudson Pacific Properties (HPP) (+26.09% W/W to $7.54), Ready Capital (RC) (+17.09% W/W to $1.85), Industrial Logistics Properties Trust (ILPT) (+16.43% W/W to $6.45), Smith Douglas Homes (SDHC) (-3.55% W/W to $14.39), and Transcontinental Realty Investors (TCI) (-3.15% W/W to $38.76) were the notable movers.

Here is a look at the subsector performances for the week:

data.title: DOJ is close to filing an antitrust lawsuit against egg producers: WSJ
data.description:
The Department of Justice is ready to file an antitrust case against some of the leading egg producers in the U.S., alleging that they coordinated to set prices through information sharing, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The rival producers coordinated by sharing pricing information with Expana, a service that sets benchmark prices for the industry using sales data, according to the Journal.

Shares of egg producers Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) and Vital Farms (VITL) fell in the post-market trading soon after the WSJ report on Friday.

The civil lawsuit would implicate major egg producers such as Cal-Maine (CALM) and Versova, which substantially raised prices in 2024 and 2025 due to a supply constraint driven by a bird flu outbreak.

The DOJ has yet to make a final decision on the case. It could settle with egg producers, allowing the companies to avoid the lawsuit, according to the people.

The large egg firms declined to comment on the potential case. Expana didn’t return requests seeking comments. “Egg farmers are price takers—they don’t set prices; the market does,” stated the American Egg Board, a lobby group for egg producers.

data.title: Should Altria sell off its Anheuser-Bush InBev stake? It's complicated
data.description:
Altria (MO) has an interesting asset on its balance sheet with its longtime stake in Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD).

The company has a historical connection to the beer industry that dates all the way back to 1969 and its predecessor, Philip Morris, which acquired Miller Brewing Company. The relationship with brewing took a new shape in 2002, when Miller Brewing merged with South African Breweries to form SABMiller, the world's second-largest beer company, with Philip Morris retaining a continuing interest in the new beer giant. 

Philip Morris Companies changed its name to Altria Group (MO) and kept its beer investment even though it divested other holdings, including spinning off Kraft Foods (KHC) in 2007 and Philip Morris International (PM) in 2008.

The beer investment took another dramatic turn in 2016 after Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) announced its $103B takeover of SABMiller. Following the closing, Altria (M) made subsequent purchases of Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) to take its stake to approximately 10.2%, a level at which it became entitled to foreign tax credits available in connection with dividends received from the company.

Speculation about Altria (MO) exiting its beer position picked up after a five-year lock-up restriction expired in 2021. At that time, analysts said the most likely and capital-efficient scenario was divesting the position gradually over time. A partial divestiture eventually was finally fired off in 2024 when Altria (MO) announced its intention to sell 35M shares of BUD at $61.50 per share and sell another $200M worth directly back to the beer company. 

Following the reduction of its stake in BUD to 8.1%, Altria (MO) CEO Billy Gifford noted the opportunistic move supported the tobacco giant's balance sheet. Looking ahead, the market broadly expects Altria's (MO) stake in BUD to continue to be lowered as the company increasingly defines its future around nicotine pouches, vaping, and smoke-free innovation rather than beer dividends.

Weighing in on the potential for more selling, Fitch Ratings said the fair value of BUD of close to $12.9B supports the company’s overall financial flexibility. "Proceeds from share sales could be used for business investments and a shift towards smoke-free products. However, it could become a rating concern if Altria uses proceeds solely for shareholder returns," warned the ratings agency.

Shares of Altria (MO) have rallied more than 30% in the five years since the lockup expired on selling BUD shares. The total return for investors is closer to 50% if dividends were reinvested.

data.title: Insider trades: Tim Cook shops Nike shares; Micron, Broadcom among other notable names
data.description:
A weekly overview of insider trading, highlighting significant purchases and sales by investors, directors, and executives. The transactions occurred between April 13, 2026, and April 17, 2026.

  • Nike (NKE) saw two major insider purchases this week. Tim Cook bought 25,000 shares at $42.43 per share for $1.06M, while President and CEO Elliott Hill acquired 23,660 shares at $42.27 per share for $1M, taking their holdings to 130,480 and 265,247 shares, respectively.
  • At Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), Chairman and CEO Che-Chia Wei purchased 186 shares at $57.87 each, totaling $10,764 and increasing his stake to 8.46M shares.
  • Petrobras (PBR) Chief Commercial Officer Angelica Laureano acquired 1,000 shares at $9.49 per share for $9,490, bringing holdings to 3,659 shares.
  • ChargePoint (CHPT) President and CEO Richard Wilmer bought 46,847 shares at $5.34 each, amounting to $249,999 and lifting his total stake to 511,224 shares.
  • Micron Technology (MU) EVP and Chief Business Officer Sumit Sadana sold 24,000 shares at $421.35 per share, generating $10M and reducing his direct holdings by 9.68% to 224,021 shares.
  • Broadcom (AVGO) director Gayla Delly disposed of 1,000 shares, trimming her direct holdings by 3%. She sold them for a total value of $358,310, reducing her stake to 32,352 shares.
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) EVP and Chief of Operations John Laughter sold 15,000 shares at $71.61 per share, totaling $1.07M and cutting his direct holdings by 21.62% to 54,369 shares.
  • Dell Technologies (DELL) CFO David Kennedy offloaded 19,500 shares at $182.53 per share for $3.56M, reducing his direct stake by 9.63% to 183,097 shares.
  • Argan (AGX) director William Leimkuhler sold 8,444 shares at $598.49 per share, generating $5.05M and lowering his holdings by 16.91% to 41,495 shares.
